summary: Enforce the literal shared-UI platform boundary against browser-adapter imports, including erased type-only edges.
specSections: AC-13.a zero direct platform-specific UI imports

decisions.topic: Literal browser-platform import enforcement in shared UI
decisions.decision: Treat any shared App/UI import whose module path names platform-web as a platform-boundary violation, including import type declarations that TypeScript erases.
decisions.rationale: AC-13.a requires zero direct platform-specific UI imports, not merely zero runtime edges. The prior scanner covered Tauri, desktop-shell, and platform-desktop but silently accepted browser implementation types, allowing shared UI contracts to become coupled to one adapter.

testEvidence.criterion: A shared UI file cannot import a browser adapter even when the declaration is type-only, while the production tree remains platform-clean.
testEvidence.test: Create a temporary shared-UI fixture with an import type edge into platform-web, require the exact platform-boundary finding, then run the real repository scan.

red.outcome: RED: 1 new regression failed and 58 existing tests passed because the scanner returned no violation for a browser-platform type import.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/source-policy.test.ts; pnpm.cmd exec biome check scripts/lib/source-policy.mjs tests/scripts/source-policy.test.ts; node scripts/check-source-policy.mjs; git diff --check
green.outcome: GREEN: all 59 focused source-policy tests passed in 1.01 seconds; scoped Biome checked both files with zero diagnostics; the production source-policy and diff gates exited 0.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
